title: How Chatbots Think
modules: Where Do the Words Come From?
Give the Bot a Job to Do
synopsis: A curious kid stumbles into a behind-the-scenes tour of a chatbot factory, where the machines can talk but nobody has explained how â and it is your job to figure it out by running experiments, making choices, and fixing things when they go hilariously wrong. This wonder-driven adventure builds real intuition for how language models work through play, not explanation.
rationale: Young children are interacting with chatbots through toys, homework helpers, and voice assistants but have no mental model for what is actually happening, leaving them unable to form healthy expectations or notice when something has gone wrong.
learning_outcome: Students can explain in their own words that a chatbot predicts words based on patterns in text it has seen, demonstrate this by constructing a simple example, and identify one situation where a chatbot would likely give a wrong or silly answer.

nearest_existing: The Most Consequential Tool Built Without a Blueprint
